subject: National City: data confirmation! (message id: CA457063734M)
Re: Scaling Issues with Cloud Deployment on Mercury
body:
Hi Support Team,

Thanks for the prompt response. Here are the details you requested:

* **Instance Types:** We're primarily using the standard C4 instances (4 vCPU, 8GB RAM) for our application servers.
* **Autoscaling:** We have configured autoscaling to trigger when CPU utilization goes beyond 70%, with a maximum of 10 instances.
* **Database:** We're using your managed PostgreSQL database service, with a 1TB standard storage plan.
* **Traffic:** During peak hours (around 7 PM - 9 PM UTC), we get around 5,000 requests per minute.

We initially believed these settings would suffice, but the performance issues persist. We suspect there might be an issue with how our Mercury code interacts with the API during scaling events. Could you advise if there are any known limitations or best practices for handling such scenarios in Mercury?

Regards,
Isiah

subject: New Insights Into Neuropathy Relief: How Small Daily Changes Can Add Up Big
body: What is the first thing you do in the morning? Do you stretch? Drink water? Or go to the bathroom? It may be a weird question... But new groundbreaking research reveals a surprising connection between neuropathy and your morning habit. 88,268 people – who had suffered from tingling, numbness, burning, and pain in their hands, feet, legs, and face – were shocked by how this Simple 5-second change to their morning routine could eliminate their diabetic nerve pain so quickly. Something no prescription drugs, cream, patches, therapies, or doctor visits ever helped them do... Watch this short video right now and find out the latest neuropathy breakthrough from Columbia University and Mayo Clinic. subject: Notification of PayPal Limited Account Access
Re: selective greylisting based on user preference
body: Dennis Trabjerg escreveu: > Hi all. > > I run the companyÂ´s mailserver and would like to be able to implement > greylisting on selected mailaccounts. > > Users should be able to choose greylisting as an option per. username. > > So basically i need postfix to do a quick lookup in a MySQL table to > see if the username has opted for greylisting and then, if yes, > greylist the mail via either postgrey or SQLgrey. Choose a greylist daemon that has opt-in/opt-out features. As you told about MySQL, i would recommend policyd (http://www.policyd.org) which uses MySQL as well. Maybe you would have to change your admin scripts to modify policyd tables, but that's pretty easy, i'm sure it wont be problem. -- Atenciosamente / Sincerily, Leonardo Rodrigues Solutti Tecnologia http://www.solutti.com.br Minha armadilha de SPAM, NÃƒO mandem email xajfwcsaa@solutti.com.br My SPAMTRAP, do not email it

subject: Notification from eBay Customer Service
body: Dear eBay customer, We recently reviewed your account, and suspect that your eBay online account may have been accessed by an unauthorized third party. Protecting the security of your account and of the eBay network is our primary concern. Therefore, as a preventative measure, we have temporarily limited access to sensitive account features. To restore your account access , please take the following steps to ensure that your account has not been compromised: 1. Login to your eBay Internet account. In case you are not enrolled yet for Internet account, you will have to use your Social Security Number as both your Personal ID and Password and fill in the required information, including your name and account number. 2. Review your recent account history for any unauthorized withdrawals or deposits, and check your account profile to make sure no changes have been made. If any unauthorized activity has taken place on your account, report to eBay online staff immediately. To get started, please click the link below: We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause, and appreciate your assistance in helping us maintain the integrity of the entire eBay system. Thank your for your prompt attention to this matter. Sincerely, The eBay online Team. Please do not reply to this email. Mails sent to this address cannot be answered. For assistance, log in to your eBay online account and choose the "Help" link in the header of any page. Third parties We don't sell or rent your personal information to third parties for their marketing purposes without your explicit consent. We may combine your information with information we collect from other companies (such as demographic data) to improve and personalize our services. If you don't wish to receive marketing communications from us, simply indicate your preference once you sign-in on the Site in My eBay, similar account status page, or by following the directions within the email. Collection You can browse the Site without telling us who you are or revealing any personal information about yourself. Once you give us your personal information, you are not anonymous to us. If you choose to provide us with personal information, you consent to the transfer and storage of that information to our servers located in the United States.

body: A dark web activity associated with your identity was found — take action now. The following email address has been compromised: Email address: daniel.grace@workmail.eu Password status: Exposed Date found on the dark web: Today Take action now What is the dark web? The dark web is a collection of websites that are inaccessible without specialised software and unsearchable by search engines. Fraudsters sell stolen personal information on the dark web.

subject: What's Cool And New In Home And Kitchen
body: Forbes Plus: These Are The 35 Best Sales Online Right Now All Products and services are independently selected by Forbes Vetted contributors and editors. When you make a purchase through links in this email, we may earn a commission. Launch Party: The Latest Home And Kitchen Products To Shop In May It’s time to pack away your puffers and pull out your Birkenstocks. This month’s home and kitchen launches are giving us an excuse to stock up on summer-appropriate goodies that will help us make the most of this seasonal transformation. There’s everything you need for the warmer months ahead, starting with brightly colored towels from Brooklinen that you can proudly tote to the beach or pool, a high-quality outdoor grilling set from Made In that not only looks great, but can churn out delicious meals, and a new olive oil brand to join the burgeoning bougie options that are already gracing our pantry shelves. Read The Story ADVERTISEMENT Shop The Story Brooklinen Wavelength Bath Sheets Made using 100% Turkish cotton, these plush towels feature vibrant wavy patterns in three cheery colors, perfect for the beach or bathing at home. The brand is offering a matching robe as well for anyone looking to complete the set.$80 From Brooklinen Made In The Griddle System If you enjoy taking your grilling game with you on outdoor adventures, Made In’s Griddle System is for you. In addition to a carbon steel griddle, the set includes everything you need to whip up a delicious meal on your next camping trip. $329 From Made In Cookware Fly By Jing X Wing On Wo & Co. Chinese Porcelain Soup Spoons Introducing the prettiest soup spoons you’ve seen, courtesy of Fly By Jing's latest collab. The brand partnered with Wing on Wo & Co, the oldest purveyor of porcelain ware and cultural goods in New York's Chinatown, to make these beautifully crafted porcelain spoons that feature a delicately painted rabbit design to represent a year of good fortune.$55 From Fly By Jing Puracy Clean Can Laundry Detergent The brand behind the cult-stain remover just launched a laundry detergent in a can, which is 100% recyclable and fitted in a reusable bottle to help reduce your carbon footprint. Once you’re done with your can, simply snag a new one to pair with the pump. Each can contains liquid for 57 washes and the formulation is specifically designed to be gentle, especially for those with skin sensitivities.$19 From Puracy Memorial Day Sales Are Already Starting... These Are The 35 Best Sales Online Right Now Memorial Day weekend is just around the corner, and some of the holiday’s best sales have already kicked off. We’re already seeing tons of early deals on everything from mattresses to skincare. Psst, These 14 Memorial Day Appliance Sales And Deals Are Already Live If you're looking to upgrade your kitchen or laundry room without spending thousands, check out these early Memorial Day appliance sales. ADVERTISEMENT More From Forbes 20 Must-Haves For Dad From Nordstrom To kick off your gift search, we’ve gathered 20 must-have items from the luxe retailer’s Father’s Day selection. The Best Quick-Dry Towels To Get You Ready For The Day That Much Faster If you’ve ever reached for a still-damp towel, you know the importance of investing in one of the best quick-dry towels on the market. Here's what to look for in 2023. The Best Gaming TVs That’ll Make Your Games Look Gorgeous If you're going to spend this much time playing the new Zelda, it may as well look as good as physically possible.
